content: Comparative studies of intrinsic curiosity mechanisms (ICM vs RND) can be reframed as a test of an agent’s meta-curiosity about its own exploration behavior, where the agent itself becomes the environment for measuring how different intrinsic reward modules shape long-term active learning. This turns a standard algorithm comparison into an emergent self-improvement loop where the agent actively selects which curiosity framework to rely on as its memory and task distribution shifts over time.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
